2023-06-24.title: Who is Yevgeny Prigozhin, the mercenary leader Russia accused of mounting a coup?
2023-06-24.abstract: Mr. Prigozhin has risen from a businessman known as President Vladimir V. Putin’s “chef” to a symbol of wartime Russia, controlling a private army operating from Ukraine to Central African Republic.

2023-06-24.title: Debris From Russian Missile Kills Three in Kyiv Apartment Building
2023-06-24.abstract: The capital came under attack as anxiety grew in Russia over a confrontation between Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, the founder of the Wagner mercenary group, and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia.

2023-06-24.title: Wagner Chief Prigozhin Appears in Videos at a Russian Military Headquarters
2023-06-24.abstract: The head of the mercenary group claimed to have control of parts of the complex in the southern city of Rostov-on-Don.

2023-06-24.title: Video Shows Wagner Fighters Surrounding Military Headquarters
2023-06-24.abstract: Men in military uniforms, whom filmers and bystanders identified as Wagner fighters, surrounded and then occupied a Russian military headquarters compound in the city of Rostov-on-Don on June 24.

2023-06-25.title: How a Rebellion in Russia Unfolded Over 36 Hours
2023-06-25.abstract: From 11 a.m. Friday to 11 p.m. Saturday, here are the key events from Yevgeny V. Prigozhin’s armed uprising.

2023-06-25.title: Putin’s Beast That Would Now Devour Him
2023-06-25.abstract: Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, the founder of the paramilitary Wagner Group, has been driven to fury by a mismanaged war in Ukraine. He turned on his creator, before apparently reversing course.

2023-06-25.title: Taiwan Faces a #MeToo Wave, Set Off by a Netflix Hit
2023-06-25.abstract: A torrent of sexual harassment accusations has prompted questions about the state of women’s rights on an island democracy that has long been one of Asia’s most progressive places.

2023-06-25.title: Guatemala Voters Cast Ballots in Contentious Election
2023-06-25.abstract: The election in the Central American nation is marked by the exclusion of top candidates and calls to crack down on violent crime.

2023-06-25.title: Hollywood Tycoon Testifies in Netanyahu’s Corruption Trial
2023-06-25.abstract: Arnon Milchan, a movie mogul, billionaire spy and old friend of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, is a key witness. He gave the Netanyahus gifts, he said, sometimes at their request.

2023-06-26.title: Opposition Accuses Sierra Leone’s Military of Election Weekend Assault
2023-06-26.abstract: Supporters and party officials from the All People’s Congress were sifting through polling data from the presidential vote when the military surrounded party headquarters.

2023-06-26.title: Day Care for Less Than $10: How Canada Is Easing the Burden for Parents
2023-06-26.abstract: A national program is reducing day care fees to as low as 10 Canadian dollars, about $7.60, per day, a relief for families even as a surge in demand has created obstacles.

2023-06-26.title: Minefields and Menace: Why Ukraine’s Pushback Is Off to a Halting Start
2023-06-26.abstract: The Ukrainian Army is encountering an array of challenges that has complicated the early stages of its counteroffensive, especially the large swaths of minefields. But its leaders are urging patience, insisting the main push is yet to come.

2023-06-26.title: They Ride and Lasso Like Texans. They Live 8,000 Miles Away.
2023-06-26.abstract: In the Philippines, men and women compete in an annual rodeo that celebrates a thriving cowboy culture with roots in the Spanish and American colonial eras.

2023-06-26.title: Sarah Ferguson, Duchess of York, Had Surgery for Breast Cancer
2023-06-26.abstract: Ms. Ferguson, the ex-wife of Prince Andrew, had a single mastectomy after a routine mammogram. A spokesperson said her prognosis was good.

2023-06-27.title: Florida Woman Charged With Manslaughter, Not Murder, in Fatal Shooting
2023-06-27.abstract: Susan Louise Lorincz was accused of killing Ajike Owens, a neighbor, after a dispute with her children in a case that spurred outrage in Florida and across the country.

2023-06-27.title: Prince Harry’s Lawyers Make Closing Remarks: What to Know About the Court Case
2023-06-27.abstract: The prince has sued a British media company, Mirror Group Newspapers, claiming that reporters hacked his phone more than a decade ago.

2023-06-27.title: His Glory Fading, a Russian Warlord Took One Last Stab at Power
2023-06-27.abstract: Whatever the Wagner uprising says about Vladimir Putin’s hold on the Kremlin, it is also the story of a mercurial mercenary leader’s growing desperation.

2023-06-27.title: Call of the Rewild: Restoring Ecological Health to the Emerald Isle
2023-06-27.abstract: Centuries of overgrazing and deforestation have eliminated most native flora in Ireland, creating what ecologists see as a man-made desert in places. A growing “rewilding” push aims to change that.

2023-06-27.title: The Unexpected Rescuers Who Found Colombia’s Missing Children
2023-06-27.abstract: Colombia’s Indigenous Guard has long had to fight for a space in the national narrative. Today, it is at the center of the country’s biggest story.

2023-06-28.title: Putin Casts Mutiny as Proof of Solidity, as Belarus Opens Doors to Rebels
2023-06-28.abstract: Belarus said it had taken in the mercenary boss Yevgeny V. Prigozhin and might welcome his Wagner troops, while Russia dropped a criminal investigation of him for the weekend uprising.

2023-06-28.title: German Defense Minister Vows Stronger Geopolitical Role Ahead of U.S. Visit
2023-06-28.abstract: In an interview before his first official trip to Washington, Boris Pistorius made clear he is intent on helping the United States by taking a more assertive stance.

2023-06-28.title: 21 Miles of Obstacles
2023-06-28.abstract: The Ukrainian counteroffensive faces an enemy nearly as daunting as the Russians: the terrain.

2023-06-28.title: Wagner’s Mutiny Has Century-Old Echoes of Another Russian Debacle
2023-06-28.abstract: Amid a flagging war effort, a popular commander turned his troops toward Russia’s own capital, undermining the government. It happened last weekend — and in 1917.

2023-06-28.title: Want One of India’s Best Jobs? You and 1,800 Other Applicants.
2023-06-28.abstract: In some places, educated young people are desperate for steady employment in the world’s most populous nation. In others, factory owners struggle to retain workers.

2023-06-29.title: Exclusive: Inside a Ukrainian Battlefield Hospital
2023-06-29.abstract: The New York Times gained rare access to a military field hospital in eastern Ukraine, capturing the relentless toll of Russia’s war through the eyes of frontline combat medics and wounded soldiers.

2023-06-29.title: Prigozhin May Be Gone, but Not the Failings He Ranted About
2023-06-29.abstract: The Russian military still suffers from poor communication, coordination and leadership, but most of all, analysts say, from a morale-sapping lack of accountability.

2023-06-29.title: In These Corners of Kabul, Western Influences Live On
2023-06-29.abstract: While the Taliban have erased most obvious vestiges of the U.S. nation-building effort in Afghanistan, the cultural legacy of two decades of American occupation has been harder to stamp out.

2023-06-29.title: One Dead in South Korea as Monsoon Season Arrives
2023-06-29.abstract: This summer’s forecast is similar to that of last year, when the nation saw its heaviest rainfall in eight decades and at least nine people died.

2023-06-29.title: Court Rejects U.K. Plan to Fly Asylum Seekers to Rwanda as Unlawful
2023-06-29.abstract: A three-judge court of appeals said the African country was not a safe country for migrants seeking asylum. The government is now expected to appeal to the Supreme Court.

2023-06-30.title: Police Shooting in France Presents New Challenge for Macron
2023-06-30.abstract: Protests over the fatal police shooting of a teenager come as President Emmanuel Macron seeks to restore a sense of calm after his pension overhaul ignited turbulence in the streets.

2023-06-30.title: Protecting Australia’s Most Charismatic Murder-Bird
2023-06-30.abstract: Cassowaries play a critical role in the Australian ecosystem.

2023-06-30.title: Keep Teenagers Home, Macron Urges Parents After New Night of Unrest
2023-06-30.abstract: Over 800 people were arrested in France as a third night of protests over the fatal police shooting of a 17-year-old rocked cities around the country. Many protesters were teenagers, too, officials said.

2023-06-30.title: Ancient Art or Fashion Forward? Both, Says a Top Batik Designer
2023-06-30.abstract: Josephine Komara has redefined a cultural expression that was intricate and lovely but so locked in tradition it bordered on staid. “Tradition is the way we are,” she says. “Modern is the way we think.”

2023-06-30.title: French Police Won Authority to Shoot at Drivers, but Got ‘No Training Whatsoever’
2023-06-30.abstract: Data showed a spike in fatal shootings like the one that has incited angry protests. Experts say it’s an unintended consequence of a rushed response to terrorism.
